speaker: Narrator
text:
A dried and refreshed Christina walks into her bedroom and picks some clothes from the clean pile. The smell isn’t too bad. She sits down at her computer. 
	
	The room is covered in Pasaya merch. The monitor’s got a few stickers taped to the back. She has casettes and an album on one shelf. Even her guitar is purple to match Pasaya's color.

	She met a fan online once who she befriended and gave her a bunch of handcrafted plushies.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Regarding her project, Christina had a pile of printouts. One half was of various diseases affecting the common roach in the Baltic Sea during the warmer months of the year.

    The other half was split between old coursework and mail. She sat down in her grease-stained office chair and popped a hard candy into her mouth. 

	She opens up Pasaya's page to see if she was following through on the early morning weekend streams this time. It was always a crapshoot whether or not she'd follow through.

speaker: Christina
text: No!

speaker: Christina
text: She promised she’d be doing a full week...

speaker: Christina
text: To the playlists again I guess. Why do you let the drones starve Pasaya?

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina emails Blanche a rough draft of their presentation alongside a couple of fish pictures with detailed notes. They proceeded to go back and forth with material and findings.

speaker: Christina
text: Fuck me, revising alone will take the whole weekend.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I've never seen handwriting in a game this bad before. Devs please fix!

speaker: Christina
text: I remember how badly she struggled this playthrough.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Now what the fuck does it say?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Right, wight, weft... UGH!

speaker: Narrator
text:
Blanche had, of course, written her notes in an inscrutable mixture of Swedish, English, and shorthand of both.
	
	Some of which she was kind enough to type up on her phone, the rest: handwritten, photographed from an awful angle, and saved with the title "-OCR".
speaker: Narrator
text: Then there were the pictures. Blanche performed the remarkable feat of making a phone with a 4k camera produce a blurry image. It was probably the water.

speaker: Christina
text: Hold on...

speaker: Christina
text: Then she dissected one?

speaker: Christina
text: That thing wasn't just living in it's head.

speaker: Narrator
text:
The segmented body of an annelid has tentacle-like appendages, some of which seem to have intertwined themselves with various organs of the fish. It was also distressingly long. 
	
	Long enough to wrap around the exterior length of the fish it lived inside.

speaker: Christina
text: If I can decipher the hieroglyphs, maybe I'll get a better understanding of what she's talking about.

speaker: Narrator
text: One of the few good shots Blanche had gotten contained a vibrant image of a white appendage peeking out from the fish's underside.

speaker: Christina
text: It's in the eye and the ass at the same time?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Look how the long bastard is.

speaker: Pasaya
text: What do you think it's eating in there?

speaker: Narrator
text:
Parasites disgusted Christina as would they would to any normal person. However, she had a strange morbid curiosity towards them. It's what got her into researching them in the first place.
	
	She found it intriguing how efficiently they evolved to nourish themselves, and how specific kinds could essentially take over the minds and bodies of insects, just so that they could spread more offspring.

speaker: Christina
text: Well, we don't know if this is an obligate parasite. If it was, we don't know if it was a parasitoid. Those were very rare in the water.

speaker: Christina
text: Which stage of its life cycle is it in?

speaker: Christina
text: I'm going to read deeper into her notes.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Well, I think it's an adult worm.

speaker: Christina
text: What the hell would you know about Parasitology?

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina swallows the peach-flavored piece of candy in her mouth and nearly gags. She switches windows to her running Pasaya playlist. It'd been automatically paused for inactivity. 
	
	For how long, it hadn't really occurred to her. It might not have even started.

speaker: Christina
text: I must be hallucinating.

speaker: Narrator
text:
She puts another piece of candy in her mouth and sets up one of the 12-hour compilations of Pasaya laddering in her favorite fighting game. 
	
	Pasaya made it her specialty to use high-risk, high-reward characters since she liked playing aggressively even if the character wasn't that viable. 
	
	There was nothing more satisfying than pulling out a win through the pure skill expression, fuck the meta, fuck all conventional wisdom, it was her against the world.

speaker: Narrator
text:
For Christina, this meant that her avatar made the most movements in her excited moments. 
	
	It was particularly adorable when you could see that the woman behind the character had already gotten up from their chair to prance around their room in celebration, especially when she squeals. 
	
	This session had already been exploited of clips. In one, it highlighted the precise session that she screamed like a braying horse. 
	
	She had gotten dozens of likes since the last time she saw it.

speaker: Narrator
text: She takes a deep breath and goes back to work. Deciphering Blanche's work is hard, but there are insights to be found amongst the chicken scratches.

speaker: Narrator
text:
What Blanche could say definitively is that it is, in fact, an animal and not some kind of plant, or fungus, or whatever, in her own words. Fairly useless to Christina, but a start. 
	
	She could see it would take much more time to specify if it was a true worm, a crustacean, a misshapen fish, mollusk, or an arthropod of any kind. 
	
	It at least contained one brain, a highly developed one.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Feel that, that's the feeling of the heel pressing through the back of your skull, whelp.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Isn't it sharp? Do you want me to force it in harder? Just say so, jackass!

speaker: Christina
text: Heh.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Blanche cites a paper she found about convergent evolution in the forms of parasitic life forms in the waters of the Baltic Sea tributaries in and surrounding Swedish coasts. 
	
	A monk founding a colony of sorts had discovered a particular parasite.

speaker: Christina
text: It was found inside of a female corpse who was drowned for her participation in mass poisonings and Satanic rituals.

speaker: Christina
text: Impressive.

speaker: Pasaya
text: You underestimated me didn't you? You, in your ignorance, could never measure my strength.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Larval runts like you, I devour them, wholly, every day, effortlessly.

speaker: Pasaya
text: You're so easy.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina takes a hard bite on her peach candy. It still remains barely intact.
	
	It's unclear when she became attracted to cartoon characters. She wasn't particularly into anime or manga until one of her high school girlfriends forced her to watch and read some,  
	
	She told herself she wasn't a weeaboo or a otaku just to be stoic and not reveal the embarrassing truth. Parasyte particularly resonanted with her though.

speaker: Pasaya
text: That's your mix-up?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Pulling that shit on me, the queen of this world.

speaker: Christina
text: Hmmm.

speaker: Pasaya
text: You're unworthy of being my toy.

speaker: Christina
text: God, why?

speaker: Pasaya
text: All you are fit for is being food, not for me. my larvae.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina's candy shatters between her teeth as she takes another hard bite, the pieces provide a boost in flavor from all the extra surface area. 
	
	She takes one hand off the keyboard and onto her crotch.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I'll fill you. I'll fill you with enough eggs to make your body swollen, just watch.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I'll watch as my children eat your organs slowly.

speaker: Pasaya
text: You'll live long enough to see them emerge from the hollowed husk of a body so that I can.

speaker: Christina
text: I-I shouldn't do this here.
